title: Synthetic variant-annotation teaching table + exercises
context: Variant annotation is a core bioinformatics skill for cancer genomics, but real somatic variant datasets often require controlled access (dbGaP/EGA) or come from NC-licensed databases like COSMIC/OncoKB. A synthetic variant-annotation table — generated with plausible variant types, gene names from open-access references, predicted functional impacts, and population frequencies from open-tier sources — teaches variant interpretation skills without touching controlled-access or NC data. Risk tier is medium because variant annotation references (e.g. COSMIC gene names, ClinVar) require careful license triage via policy-003. Calibration sources must be from the approved catalog. Depends on catalog-010 and synthesis-004.
objective: Produce a synthetic variant-annotation teaching table generated from published aggregate variant characteristics and open-access reference gene sets, with full gate artifact, exercises, and Zenodo DOI.
acceptanceCriteria: Synthetic variant table generated from a seed + parameters provenanced to open-access aggregate variant/gene references; no NC-licensed or controlled-access data used as input (verified via policy-003).
gate-002 PASS artifact committed; safety notice present; riskTier:medium (due to license judgment on calibration sources).

Data dictionary covers columns: variant_id, gene, chromosome, position, ref_allele, alt_allele, variant_type, predicted_impact, population_frequency, clinical_significance (synthetic label); known limitations documented.
Completeness >= 90/100; Croissant metadata valid; Zenodo DOI minted; reproducibility check = PASS.
At least 2 embedded exercises (e.g. filter by impact, classify variant types) with worked solutions.
outputLicense: CC0-1.0 (or CC-BY-4.0 if any CC-BY calibration source is used); safety reviewer and domain reviewer sign-off.
output: A synthetic variant-annotation teaching table CSV/TSV + provenance.json + gate PASS artifact + Croissant metadata + data dictionary + exercises/solutions + Zenodo DOI.
